confirmed.title: DateTimeInput ignores displayFormat/format/timezone/seconds-display props from DateTimeField
confirmed.detail: DateTimeField.php emits `displayFormat` (`d/m/Y H:i`), `format`, `timezone`, and `seconds`, but DateTimeInput's native `<input type="datetime-local">` only consumes `minDate`/`maxDate` and `seconds` (as the `step` value, line 27). `displayFormat`, `format`, and `timezone` are dead props: the datetime-local control renders in the browser locale/zone, so a panel that declares `->timezone('America/Sao_Paulo')` on a DateTimeField sees the raw UTC value in the editor. Mirrors the DateInput gap.
confirmed.why_real:
Verified against source. DateField::getTypeSpecificProps() (packages/fields/src/Types/DateField.php:127-137) serializes format, displayFormat, and timezone props, and DateTimeField adds seconds (DateTimeField.php:49-55). The PHP docblock (DateField.php:17-19) documents timezone() as "lets the field declare the user's timezone for display conversion." However DateTimeInput.tsx (packages-js/fields-js/src/date/DateTimeInput.tsx:14-31) consumes only minDate (line 25), maxDate (line 26), and seconds-as-step (line 27). displayFormat, format, and timezone are dead props: the native <input type="datetime-local"> renders in the browser locale/zone, so a field declaring ->timezone('America/Sao_Paulo') shows the raw stored UTC value to the editor with no conversion — a genuine user-facing locale defect, not a dev-only string. This is the documented-but-unwired serialization family.

Novel vs ledger: The round-1/2/3 ledger has NO entry for DateTimeInput.tsx or its sibling DateInput.tsx. All prior date/timezone entries target the display/table/export side: table/cells.tsx DateCell (round 2), versioning/VersionTimeline.tsx (round 2), packages/table/src/Columns/DateColumn.php timezone-not-emitted (round 3), export/FormatsDateCells.php (round 3), and CurrencyField/CurrencyInput. None cover the form-input editor components in fields-js/src/date/. The finding's own note "Mirrors the DateInput gap" refers to a sibling that is also absent from the ledger, so this is not a duplicate.

confirmed.title: BooleanCell aria-label hardcodes English 'true'/'false'
confirmed.detail: The boolean cell renders `<span role="img" aria-label={truthy ? 'true' : 'false'}>`. These accessible labels are hardcoded English and never pass through the translation hook, so a screen-reader user on a pt-BR/de panel hears 'true'/'false' instead of localized 'sim'/'não'. The round-2 cells.tsx pass localized date/number formatting but left this a11y string. Not in ledger.
confirmed.why_real:
Confirmed at packages-js/ui/src/table/cells.tsx:139. BooleanCell renders `<span role="img" aria-label={truthy ? 'true' : 'false'}>` with hardcoded English accessible labels and no translation hook, while sibling cells in the same file already consume useArqelLocale for date/number formatting. The visible glyph (✓/—) is language-neutral, so the defect is confined to the accessible-name layer: a screen-reader user on a pt-BR/de panel hears "true"/"false" instead of localized "sim/não". This reproduces in any table with a boolean column.

Not a duplicate: the ledger has four round-2 cells.tsx entries (lines 153, 155, 158, 169) but all concern DateCell/NumberCell visible formatting; none touch the BooleanCell aria-label at line 139. No round covers this a11y string. The round-1 a11y sweep covered DataTable/ActionMenu/etc. aria-labels but not cells.tsx booleans.

Confidence is med rather than high because impact is narrow (screen-reader users only; sighted users see a neutral glyph) and no `form.true`/`form.false` key currently exists, so it is a smaller next-layer gap than a visible-text defect — but it is a genuine, novel, reproducing user-facing (a11y) i18n defect.

confirmed.title: NumberInput stepper buttons aria-label hardcoded English 'Increment'/'Decrement'
confirmed.detail: Both stepper buttons use `aria-label="Increment"` (line 66) and `aria-label="Decrement"` (line 74) as literal English with no t() call. Screen-reader users on a non-English panel get untranslated control names. Sibling field inputs received aria fixes in earlier rounds; these two were missed. Not in ledger.
confirmed.why_real: Verified at /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/packages-js/fields-js/src/number/NumberInput.tsx: line 66 has aria-label="Increment" and line 75 has aria-label="Decrement", both literal English with no t()/__() call. The component imports only cn and inputClasses — no translation hook is present. These are user-facing accessible control names exposed to screen readers, so on a non-English panel they render untranslated (genuine a11y-i18n defect, not a dev-only/debug string). Ledger scan (reports/i18n-fixed-ledger.json, rounds 1-3) contains no entry for NumberInput.tsx; the nearest sibling entries are CurrencyInput.tsx:64 (numeric parse locale, round 3) and cells.tsx NumberCell — neither covers these stepper aria-labels. Rounds 1-2 did wire many other aria-labels through t() (DataTable, ActionMenu, pagination, dashboard date-range inputs), confirming the "sibling fixes missed these two" claim. Real and novel.

confirmed.title: VersionTimeline hardcodes English UI strings ('Initial', 'Compare', 'Restore', 'No versions yet.', aria feed labels)
confirmed.detail: Round-2 fixed only formatRelativeTime's locale; the surrounding chrome is still raw English: `Initial` badge (line 179), `Compare` button (line 199), `Restore` button (line 210), `No versions yet.` empty state (line 141), and aria-labels `Loading versions` (line 124) / `Version history` (line 151) / the per-item `aria-label={`Version ${id} by ${userName}, ${relative}: ...`}` (line 163). None use a translation hook even though useArqelLocale is already imported. Next layer of the versioning i18n work; not in ledger.
confirmed.why_real: Verified against packages-js/versioning/src/VersionTimeline.tsx. The component imports only useArqelLocale (a BCP-47 locale string used solely for Intl.RelativeTimeFormat), not a translation hook. All cited chrome strings reproduce as raw hardcoded English with no t()/__() wiring: aria-label="Loading versions" (line 124), empty-state "No versions yet." (line 141), aria-label="Version history" (line 151), the per-item aria-label template (line 163), the "Initial" badge (line 179), the "Compare" button (line 199), and the "Restore" button (line 210). These are all user-facing visible labels and accessible names in a public shipped UI component — not dev-only/test strings. Ledger scan: the only prior VersionTimeline entry is round-2 line 36, which covers a different defect (formatRelativeTime hardcoding the 'en' locale arg to Intl.RelativeTimeFormat). The chrome/aria strings here are not in rounds 1-3. Genuinely novel next-layer i18n defect.

confirmed.title: ResetPasswordPage: every label/button/link/title hardcoded, no t()
confirmed.detail: Framework-hardcoded. The only bundled auth page NOT swept in Round 3 (Login/Register/Forgot/VerifyEmail were). All user-facing strings are hardcoded literals mixing PT-BR and English: title default 'Definir nova senha' (53), description 'Escolha uma nova senha para sua conta' (54), heroImageAlt 'Reset password illustration' (56), labels 'E-mail' (83), 'Nova senha' (99), 'Confirmar senha' (114), button 'Salvando…'/'Redefinir senha' (132), link 'Voltar ao login' (138). Component never imports useArqelTranslations. arqel.auth.* needs reset_title/reset_description/reset_submit/reset_submitting added, mirroring the Round-3 sibling-page fix.
confirmed.why_real: Verified against packages-js/auth/src/ResetPasswordPage.tsx. All user-facing strings are hardcoded literals: title='Definir nova senha' (53), description='Escolha uma nova senha para sua conta' (54), heroImageAlt='Reset password illustration' (56), labels 'E-mail' (83), 'Nova senha' (99), 'Confirmar senha' (114), button 'Salvando…'/'Redefinir senha' (132), link 'Voltar ao login' (138). The component imports only from @arqel-dev/ui and @inertiajs/react — it never imports useArqelTranslations, so none of these strings can route through the translation dictionary. Defaults also mix PT-BR and English, the exact anti-pattern fixed in the Round-3 sibling pages. These are visible UI strings (not dev-only/aria-only). Ledger check: i18n-fixed-ledger.json round-3 entries cover LoginPage.tsx:55, RegisterPage.tsx:49, ForgotPasswordPage.tsx:49, VerifyEmailNoticePage.tsx:31 — the four other bundled auth pages — but ResetPasswordPage.tsx appears in NO ledger entry (rounds 1, 2, or 3). It is the single bundled auth page omitted from the Round-3 sweep. Genuine, novel, user-facing i18n defect.

confirmed.title: AI field default button labels hardcoded English (Generate/Classify/Translate/Extract/Analyze with AI + Regenerate)
confirmed.detail: Framework-hardcoded. The @arqel-dev/ai field package uses NO translation hook anywhere (grep returns nothing). Default visible button labels are module constants: AiTextInput DEFAULT_BUTTON_LABEL='Generate with AI' (57), REGENERATE_LABEL='Regenerate' (58); AiSelectInput 'Classify with AI' (72); AiExtractInput fallback 'Extract with AI' (147); AiImageInput fallback 'Analyze with AI' (162). These render whenever the server omits buttonLabel, so a pt_BR admin sees English. No arqel.ai.* dictionary section exists.
confirmed.why_real: VERIFIED REAL and NOVEL. Reproduces on disk: (1) JS side — packages-js/ai/src/AiTextInput.tsx:57-58 has module constants DEFAULT_BUTTON_LABEL='Generate with AI' and REGENERATE_LABEL='Regenerate'; AiSelectInput.tsx:72 'Classify with AI'; AiExtractInput.tsx:147 fallback 'Extract with AI'; AiImageInput.tsx:162 fallback 'Analyze with AI'. grep for useArqelTranslations/useTranslation/__( across packages-js/ai/src returns nothing — the AI field package wires no translation hook at all. (2) PHP side reinforces it: packages/ai/src/Fields/AiTextField.php:40 (and AiExtractField.php:49) set $buttonLabel = 'Generate/Extract with AI' as hardcoded English and serialize it verbatim in toArray() (AiTextField.php:188, AiExtractField.php:225) — never through __(). No arqel.ai.* dictionary section exists. Net effect: a pt_BR admin who does not manually override ->buttonLabel(__('...')) sees English labels (Generate/Regenerate/Classify/Extract/Analyze with AI). These are user-visible button labels in production forms, not dev-only strings. NOVEL: the i18n-fixed-ledger.json (rounds 1-3) contains zero entries for the @arqel-dev/ai or packages/ai packages — the closest analogues (auth pages r3, Field::$label r1, Action::$label r1, Widget r2) are distinct files/packages. Same systemic family (documented-but-unwired field-label serialization + no JS t()), but this specific package/component is uncovered.

confirmed.title: AI field visible action text hardcoded ('Apply', 'Apply all', 'Translate all missing', 'Translate from {x}', 'Source: {x}')
confirmed.detail: Framework-hardcoded. Visible English strings with no t(): AiExtractInput 'Apply all' (268) and per-row 'Apply' (302) and 'Source: {sourceField}' (239); AiImageInput 'Apply all' (358) and per-key 'Apply'; AiTranslateInput 'Translate all missing' (272) and 'Translate from {defaultLanguage}' (335). End-user admin-panel controls.
confirmed.why_real: Verified against /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/packages-js/ai/src/AiExtractInput.tsx. The hardcoded English strings reproduce exactly: line 239 `<Label>Source: {sourceField}</Label>`, line 268 `Apply all` button, line 302 per-row `Apply` button, and line 300 `aria-label={`Apply ${target}`}`. A grep over the entire packages-js/ai/src/ directory found ZERO usage of any translation mechanism (no t(), no __(), no useArqelTranslations import) — the AiExtractInput.tsx file imports only @arqel-dev/ui primitives and react. These are end-user admin-panel controls (preview-apply UI for AI-extracted field values), not dev-only/debug strings. Novel: the rounds 1-3 ledger (i18n-fixed-ledger.json) covers ui, auth, core, fields, table, nav, actions, widgets, export, and versioning packages but contains NO entry for any packages-js/ai/ file — the AI field components were never audited. This is directly analogous to the already-confirmed round-3 auth LoginPage entry ('every label/button hardcoded, no translation hook'). The finding's broader scope (AiImageInput, AiTranslateInput) is plausible given the package-wide absence of translation usage, though only AiExtractInput.tsx was line-verified here; the verified portion alone establishes a real, novel, user-facing i18n defect.

confirmed.title: AI field user-facing error strings hardcoded English
confirmed.detail: Framework-hardcoded. setError() passes raw English shown in the field error banner: AiSelectInput 'Classification failed (HTTP n).' (183), 'Could not classify.' (203), 'Classification failed: network error.' (205); AiTextInput 'Generation failed (HTTP n).' (169), 'Generation failed: network error.' (178); AiTranslateInput 'Translation failed (HTTP n).' (211), 'invalid response body.' (218), 'network error.' (225); AiExtractInput 'Extraction failed (HTTP n).' (216). The 'Missing X URL' messages are dev-facing config errors; the failure/network ones reach end users.
confirmed.why_real:
Verified and confirmed real + novel.

REPRODUCES: In packages-js/ai/src/AiSelectInput.tsx the runtime error state (useState at L137) is set with hardcoded English literals at L183 ('Classification failed (HTTP n).'), L203 ('Could not classify.'), and L205 ('Classification failed: network error.'). That `error` value is rendered to end users at L282-284 inside a destructive <Alert><AlertDescription>{error}</AlertDescription>. The same pattern holds in the sibling files cited: AiTextInput.tsx (L169, L178; rendered L224-225), AiTranslateInput.tsx (L211, L218, L225; rendered L342-343), AiExtractInput.tsx (L216, L222, L228; rendered L318-319).

USER-FACING: The failure/HTTP/network strings fire on transient runtime server/network errors during normal end-user operation and are shown in the field-level error banner, so they are genuinely user-facing. (The 'Missing X URL' strings are dev-facing config errors from missing props and are correctly excluded by the finding.)

NOT ALREADY WIRED: grep across the entire packages-js/ai/src tree shows zero useArqelTranslations / t() / __() usage — no translation hook exists in this package, so these are unambiguously hardcoded.

NOVEL: The ledger (rounds 1-3) covers packages-js/ui, packages-js/auth, packages-js/fields-js, packages-js/versioning, packages/nav, packages/core, and other PHP packages, but contains NO packages-js/ai/* entry. Not a duplicate.

confirmed.title: AI status sr-only/aria labels hardcoded (Analyzing/Classifying/Extracting/Generating/Translating)
confirmed.detail: Framework-hardcoded. role=status live-region aria-labels announce progress in English: AiTextInput 'Generating' (215); AiImageInput 'Analyzing' (343) + 'Image file' (315); AiSelectInput 'Classifying' (241) + SelectValue placeholder 'Select...' (219); AiExtractInput 'Extracting' (253); AiTranslateInput 'Translating' (331) + 'Missing translation' (301). No translation hook in package.
confirmed.why_real:
Verified at the source. packages-js/ai/src/AiTextInput.tsx:215 renders `<span role="status" aria-label="Generating">` as an ARIA live region while a generation request is in flight — a user-facing accessibility string announced verbatim in English by screen readers regardless of panel locale. The same hardcoded pattern is confirmed across the sibling AI input components: AiImageInput.tsx (aria-label="Analyzing" at 343, aria-label="Image file" at 315), AiSelectInput.tsx (aria-label="Classifying" at 241, plus a VISIBLE SelectValue placeholder="Select..." at 219), AiExtractInput.tsx (aria-label="Extracting" at 253), and AiTranslateInput.tsx (aria-label="Translating" at 331, aria-label="Missing translation" at 301). These are production render-path strings (the Select placeholder is visible text, the role=status labels are SR-announced), not dev-only/debug strings.

Adversarial checks all fail to refute: (1) grep for useArqelTranslations/useTranslation/t(/__( across the entire packages-js/ai/src/ tree returns ZERO hits — there is no translation hook wired anywhere in the package, so these cannot be already-localized. (2) The i18n-fixed-ledger.json (rounds 1-3) contains NO entry referencing packages-js/ai/ — rounds covered ui, react, core, fields, table, actions, nav, widgets, export, auth, versioning, but never the ai package. So this is novel, not a duplicate.

Genuine user-facing i18n/a11y defect, novel relative to the ledger. Confirmed.

confirmed.title: fields-advanced editor controls: every aria-label hardcoded English (Repeater/Wizard/Builder/Markdown/RichText)
confirmed.suggested_key: new arqel.fields.* aria section: markdown_bold / repeater_move_up / wizard_next / builder_add_block
confirmed.detail: Framework-hardcoded. @arqel-dev/fields-advanced uses NO translation hook (grep confirms zero useArqelTranslations). Every control accessible name is a hardcoded English literal: MarkdownInput 'Markdown formatting' (421), 'Bold' (430), 'Italic' (439), 'Heading' (448), 'Inline code' (457), 'Link' (466), 'List' (475), 'Open preview' (486), tablist 'Editor mode' (507), 'Markdown preview' (549/560/565); RepeaterInput 'Move up' (547), 'Move down' (556), 'Add item' (626); WizardInput 'Back' (568), 'Submit' (578), 'Next' (588); BuilderInput 'Close block picker' (426), 'Add block' (431/749); RichTextInput 'Formatting toolbar' (354). The entire rich-content editing surface is English-only for screen readers.
confirmed.why_real: Reproduced. At packages-js/fields-advanced/src/markdown/MarkdownInput.tsx the cited aria-labels are hardcoded English literals: "Markdown formatting" (421), "Bold" (430), "Italic" (439), "Heading" (448), "Inline code" (457), "Link" (466), "List" (475), "Open preview" (486), tablist "Editor mode" (507), "Markdown preview" (549/560/565). Sibling components confirm the same pattern: RepeaterInput "Move up" (547)/"Move down" (556)/"Add item" (626); WizardInput "Back" (568)/"Submit" (578)/"Next" (588); BuilderInput "Close block picker" (426)/"Add block" (431/749); plus many interpolated English templates. grep for useArqelTranslations/useTranslation across packages-js/fields-advanced/src returns zero matches, confirming the package has no translation pathway — these are framework-owned strings users cannot localize, exposed as screen-reader accessible names (user-facing a11y, not dev-only). Novel: the ledger (rounds 1-3) has zero fields-advanced entries; prior rounds covered ui/auth/versioning/core/nav/table/fields/actions/export/widgets/fields-js/react only. Not a false positive — no t()/__() is wired, strings are accessibility-surface, and the package is genuinely untranslated.

confirmed.title: fields-js NumberInput/FileInput aria-labels hardcoded English
confirmed.detail: Framework-hardcoded. @arqel-dev/fields-js (core field bundle, no translation hook) hardcodes control accessible names: NumberInput 'Increment' (66) and 'Decrement' (75); FileInput dropzone 'File upload' (packages-js/fields-js/src/file/FileInput.tsx:39). Stock CRUD-form fields on every panel.
confirmed.why_real: Reproduced in source. NumberInput.tsx:66 has aria-label="Increment" and line 75 aria-label="Decrement"; FileInput.tsx:39 has aria-label="File upload" — all hardcoded English literals on stock CRUD-form field controls. The @arqel-dev/fields-js bundle imports no translation hook, so there is no wired t()/__() being bypassed; these are genuinely untranslatable. They are real user-facing screen-reader accessible names (not dev-only/debug strings), rendered on every panel's number and file fields. Novelty check against /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/reports/i18n-fixed-ledger.json (rounds 1-3): no entry for NumberInput.tsx or FileInput.tsx exists. The only fields-js entry is round-3 CurrencyInput.tsx:64, which is a different file and a different defect (en-US numeric parse path, not aria-labels). Therefore the defect is real and novel.

confirmed.title: VersionTimeline list aria-labels hardcoded ('Loading versions' / 'Version history')
confirmed.detail: Framework-hardcoded. Round 2 fixed only formatRelativeTime (line 36) in this file. Two structural aria-labels remain hardcoded English: loading-state list aria-label 'Loading versions' (124) and populated list aria-label 'Version history' (151). No translation hook used for them.
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed by reading packages-js/versioning/src/VersionTimeline.tsx. Line 124 has aria-label="Loading versions" (loading-state <ol role="feed">) and line 151 has aria-label="Version history" (populated <ol role="feed">). Both are static English literals exposed to assistive technology at runtime — genuinely user-facing a11y strings, not dev-only. The component already imports useArqelLocale (line 10) and the codebase has a translation hook, so these labels should be localized but are not wired to t()/__(). Ledger check: the only VersionTimeline.tsx entry is round 2 line 36 (formatRelativeTime hardcoding 'en' locale), a distinct concern. No ledger entry exists for lines 124 or 151 nor for these aria-labels across rounds 1-3. Novel and reproduces.

confirmed.title: Vendored shadcn Sidebar toggle a11y text hardcoded ('Toggle Sidebar' / 'Sidebar' / 'Displays the mobile sidebar.')
confirmed.detail: Framework-hardcoded (generated shadcn boilerplate, regenerable, hence low). The nav rail toggle exposes hardcoded English accessible names: SidebarTrigger sr-only 'Toggle Sidebar' (277); SidebarRail aria-label and title 'Toggle Sidebar' (289, 292); mobile sheet SheetTitle 'Sidebar' + SheetDescription 'Displays the mobile sidebar.' (199-200). It is the only accessible name for the sidebar toggle on every panel, so non-English screen-reader users hear English.
confirmed.why_real: Verified in /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/ui/sidebar.tsx: sr-only "Toggle Sidebar" (line 277), SidebarRail aria-label "Toggle Sidebar" (289) and title "Toggle Sidebar" (292), and the mobile sheet SheetTitle "Sidebar" + SheetDescription "Displays the mobile sidebar." (199-200). These are all hardcoded English with no t()/__() wiring. They are the sole accessible names for the sidebar toggle, which is rendered on every panel via the shell, so non-English screen-reader users hear English — a genuine user-facing a11y i18n defect of the exact class already accepted into the ledger (e.g. Topbar theme-toggle aria-label, Breadcrumbs aria-label="Breadcrumb", CommandPalette aria-labels). Novel: scanned the round 1-3 ledger; it has many packages-js/ui findings but NONE in sidebar.tsx (closest shell entries are Topbar.tsx, TenantSwitcher.tsx, Breadcrumbs.tsx, CommandPalette.tsx — different files). The "regenerable shadcn boilerplate / low severity" caveat is mitigating but does not negate the defect: as shipped, the accessible name is permanently English-only despite the framework having a wired translation layer used by sibling components.

confirmed.title: Resource::getPluralLabel pluralizes the ALREADY-translated label with English Str::plural()
confirmed.suggested_key: use a dedicated pluralLabel translation key; do not inflect a translated singular
confirmed.detail: Framework-hardcoded behavior. getPluralLabel() (90-95) builds the plural from getLabel() — already run through trans()/localizeLabel — then applies Str::of(...)->plural(), the English inflector. When $label resolves to a non-English noun, English plural morphology is applied to it (and the subsequent localizeLabel is a no-op since the string is no longer a key). A localized resource that only sets $label and relies on auto-plural gets a corrupted plural in sidebar/breadcrumbs. Plural should come from a dedicated pluralLabel key, not from inflecting a translated singular.
confirmed.why_real:
Code confirmed at /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/packages/core/src/Resources/Resource.php:88-95. getPluralLabel() does: $label = static::$pluralLabel ?? Str::of(static::getLabel())->plural()->toString(); then localizeLabel($label). Since getLabel() (lines 78-86) already runs the singular through localizeLabel()/trans(), the auto-derivation path inflects an ALREADY-TRANSLATED string with the English-only Str::plural() inflector. For a localized resource that sets only $label (e.g. a translation key resolving to a non-English noun like German "Benutzer" -> "Benutzers", or Portuguese nouns), the plural shown in sidebar/breadcrumbs is morphologically corrupted; the trailing localizeLabel() is a no-op because the inflected string is no longer a translation key. This reproduces in framework production code and is user-facing.

Novel vs ledger: the only related entry is round-3 InertiaDataBuilder.php:363 ("Resource::getLabel/getPluralLabel serialized verbatim - never passed through __()"), which is a DIFFERENT defect (labels not translated at all) and was evidently the basis for adding localizeLabel(). That fix does not address English-inflection of the translated singular. No ledger entry references Str::plural(), the getPluralLabel auto-derivation path, or Resource.php:90. Not dev-only, not a wired t()/__() string.

Mitigating but not refuting: the $pluralLabel property (line 43) is a documented escape hatch and can itself be a translation key, so resources CAN supply a correct localized plural. But the default auto-plural path is genuinely locale-broken, so the finding stands as a real bug.

confirmed.title: NumberInput stepper buttons: aria-label="Increment"/"Decrement" hardcoded English
confirmed.detail: The increment/decrement stepper buttons have aria-label="Increment" (line 66) and aria-label="Decrement" (line 75) hardcoded in English. fields-js already depends on @arqel-dev/ui which re-exports useArqelTranslations (precedent: Round 3 wired the hook into this same package's CurrencyInput), so this is fixable the same way. Screen-reader users in a pt_BR/es panel hear English control names. Not in the ledger (ledger covered CurrencyInput only, not NumberInput).
confirmed.why_real:
Verified against source. packages-js/fields-js/src/number/NumberInput.tsx line 66 has aria-label="Increment" and line 75 has aria-label="Decrement", both hardcoded English. These are real user-facing accessible names: a screen-reader user on a pt_BR/es panel hears English control names. Not dev-only strings, not already wired to t()/__(). Scanned the round-1/2/3 ledger (reports/i18n-fixed-ledger.json): NumberInput is not present. The round-3 CurrencyInput entry is a different file (number/CurrencyInput.tsx line 64) and a different defect (en-US comma-decimal parsing), so this is not a duplicate. Both conditions to confirm are satisfied: real user-facing i18n defect AND novel.

Caveat lowering confidence: the finding's stated rationale is partly false. It claims "Round 3 wired the hook into this same package's CurrencyInput" as a fixability precedent. I read CurrencyInput.tsx in full and grepped the whole fields-js package — useArqelTranslations is NOT used anywhere in fields-js, including CurrencyInput. Every aria-label in the package (PasswordInput, FileInput, MultiSelectInput, ColorInput, NumberInput) is hardcoded English; there is no in-package translation precedent. The fabricated precedent does not change that the defect itself genuinely reproduces, but it means the fix is a broader plumbing effort than the finding implies (the hook would need to be introduced to the package), not a trivial copy of an existing pattern.

confirmed.title: FileInput: aria-label="File upload" hardcoded English
confirmed.detail: The file input control carries aria-label="File upload" hardcoded in English. Same package (fields-js) can reach useArqelTranslations via @arqel-dev/ui. Screen-reader label is not localized for non-English panels. Not in the ledger.
confirmed.why_real:
Verified at /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/packages-js/fields-js/src/file/FileInput.tsx:39: the <section> carries aria-label="File upload" as a hardcoded English literal with no t()/__() wrapping. The component imports nothing from any translation utility, and in fact has three hardcoded English user-facing strings (line 39 aria-label, line 60 "Drag a file here or click to browse", line 63 "Browse"/"Choose another file") — none localized. This is a genuine screen-reader accessible label that stays English in non-English panels.

Reachability holds: fields-js declares @arqel-dev/ui as a peer dependency, and @arqel-dev/ui depends on @arqel-dev/react, which exports useArqelTranslations (the hook is imported across the codebase as `@arqel-dev/react/utils`). The finding's claim that the hook is reachable "via @arqel-dev/ui" is substantively correct, though slightly imprecise on the exact import path (the hook physically lives in @arqel-dev/react, re-exposed through the ui peer-dep graph). That imprecision does not affect the verdict.

Not a duplicate: the file packages-js/fields-js/src/file/FileInput.tsx appears 0 times in the rounds 1-3 ledger (grep count 0). The ledger covers ui, react, auth, versioning, fields-js/number (CurrencyInput) and PHP packages, but never the file field. The defect is the exact same class already accepted in the ledger for sibling components (FlashToast aria-label="Dismiss" r1, ChartCard aria-label="Loading chart" r1, Breadcrumbs aria-label="Breadcrumb" r1), confirming it is a recognized, real defect shape rather than a false positive. Not dev-only, not already wired.

confirmed.title: MultiSelectInput chip remove button: aria-label built from hardcoded English template `Remove ${label}`
confirmed.detail: Each selected-value chip's remove button uses aria-label={`Remove ${opt?.label ?? String(v)}`} — the verb 'Remove' is a hardcoded English template, so SR users in other locales hear 'Remove <localized label>'. Should use a parameterized translation key (e.g. t('arqel.fields.multiselect.remove', 'Remove :label', { label })). Not in the ledger.
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed at packages-js/fields-js/src/select/MultiSelectInput.tsx:50: `aria-label={`Remove ${opt?.label ?? String(v)}`}`. The verb "Remove" is a hardcoded English template with no t()/__() wiring. This is a genuine user-facing a11y/i18n defect: the aria-label is announced to screen-reader users, so non-English-locale SR users hear "Remove <localized label>" with the English verb. Verified the package has zero translation infrastructure (no useArqelTranslations import anywhere in fields-js/src, no t() in the select dir), so this is not already-wired and not a dev-only string. Not a duplicate: scanned the round-1/2/3 ledger (i18n-fixed-ledger.json) — no entry references MultiSelectInput.tsx or fields-js/src/select/; all sibling hardcoded-aria-label findings are in packages-js/ui/ (Topbar, TenantSwitcher, DataTable, ActionMenu, FlashToast, etc.), different files. Confidence is med rather than high because it is a low-severity single string and part of a package-wide absence of i18n rather than an isolated regression, but it does reproduce exactly as described and is novel.

confirmed.title: BelongsToInput: search placeholder built from hardcoded English template `Search ${relatedResource}…`
confirmed.detail: placeholder={f.placeholder ?? `Search ${f.props.relatedResource}…`} falls back to a hardcoded English 'Search <resource>…' template when the field declares no explicit placeholder. Visible to all users (not just SR). Should resolve through a parameterized key. Not in the ledger (Round 1 fixed ResourceIndex search placeholder and the BelongsTo *select* fallback placeholder in ui/nativeFields.tsx:350, but NOT this autocomplete search placeholder in fields-js/relationship/BelongsToInput.tsx).
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed real and novel. At packages-js/fields-js/src/relationship/BelongsToInput.tsx:80 the placeholder falls back to a hardcoded English template `Search ${f.props.relatedResource}…` when no explicit f.placeholder is set. This is a registered, shipping field (register.ts:37 registerField('BelongsToInput', ...)), and the placeholder is user-visible on a real <input> (not SR-only). No translation hook (useArqelTranslations/t()/__()) exists anywhere in fields-js/src/relationship/, so the English word 'Search' is unconditionally rendered for non-English locales. Ledger scan: the only BelongsTo placeholder entry is round-1 'packages-js/ui/src/form/nativeFields.tsx:350' — a DIFFERENT package (ui) and a DIFFERENT component (the native select fallback, whose BelongsToInput now begins ~line 355). The fields-js async-search autocomplete picker is not referenced in any round-1/2/3 ledger entry. Confidence is med rather than high because relatedResource is a raw resource identifier that would still interpolate an un-localized noun even with a parameterized key; nonetheless the hardcoded English 'Search' prefix is itself a clear user-facing i18n defect.
